# UC-11.7.1 · Asterisk PJSIP Trunk Registration Failure Trending

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Operations &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch this Asterisk system’s calls and health, so trunks, queues, and voicemail stay in shape for everyday use.*

---

## Description

Carrier and ITSP trunks require stable PJSIP registrations. Registration loss severs inbound DIDs and can break emergency calling without an obvious application fault.

## Value

Shortens outages affecting revenue lines, clarifies firewall versus carrier blame, and validates certificate or NAT changes.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize PJSIP lines to structured fields. (2) Ingest to `voip`. (3) Baseline hourly failures. (4) Alert on sustained elevation.

## SPL

```spl
index=voip sourcetype="asterisk:pjsip" earliest=-24h
| eval endpoint=coalesce(endpoint, Endpoint, resource, Resource, contact, Contact, "unknown")
| eval state=coalesce(state, State, status, Status, event, Event, "")
| where match(lower(state), "(?i)unavailable|reject|failed|unregistered|auth.*fail")
| timechart span=1h dc(endpoint) as affected_endpoints count as failure_events
```

## Visualization

Area chart failure_events; table of endpoints; overlay changes.

## Known False Positives

Carrier work, cert rotation on trunks, and NAT rebinds that drop registration briefly and recover.

## References

- [Asterisk Wiki](https://wiki.asterisk.org/wiki/display/AST/Documentation)
- [Splunk — Monitor network ports](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Data/Monitornetworkports)
